channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania's Tourism Sector Surpasses USD 4.41 Billion in 2025 with 2.29 Million Visitors
item.description: Tanzania's tourism sector recorded significant growth in 2025, with international arrivals reaching 2,294,495, a 7.1% increase from 2024. Tourism earnings rose to USD 4,410.6 million, up 13% from USD ...

item.title: AC/DC's 2026 North American Tour Setlist Features Classic Hits
item.description: AC/DC kicked off their 2026 North American tour in Charlotte, North Carolina, delivering a 21-song setlist packed with fan-favourites. The performance included iconic tracks such as 'Back In Black', '...

item.title: The Age publishes its Superquiz and Mini Crossword for Thursday, 16 July
item.description: This article is a promotional and index page for The Age's daily puzzle offerings rather than a traditional news report. It announces the availability of the Superquiz for Thursday, 16 July, written b...

item.title: India and UK Sign Comprehensive Trade Agreement to Boost Economic Ties
item.description: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) has officially taken effect, significantly reducing tariffs on thousands of goods and expanding market access for services between the tw...

item.title: Wildfires in Ontario Cause Air Quality Warnings and Evacuations
item.description: Smoke from wildfires in northwestern Ontario is drifting south, leading to poor air quality in Toronto and southern Ontario. Environment Canada has issued warnings that this condition may persist unti...

item.title: Political Defections and ED Summons: TMC Factional Struggles
item.description: Madan Mitra, a former TMC minister, has switched allegiance to the rebel faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee, citing the Enforcement Directorate's (ED) summons to his family as the reason. Mahua Moitra,...

item.title: Kenya Power Conducts Scheduled Maintenance with Outages in Seven Counties
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company has announced planned electricity outages across seven counties on Thursday, July 16. The maintenance work includes enhancing new connections, system upgrades, and rep...

item.title: Christian Yoka's efforts to stabilize Congo's finances
item.description: Christian Yoka, the Republic of Congo's finance minister, has taken significant steps to address the country's financial challenges. Drawing on his experience at the French Development Agency, Yoka ha...

item.title: Australian Theatre Industry Faces Struggles Amid Musical Cuts
item.description: The Australian theatre industry is experiencing significant challenges, with several globally successful musicals ending their runs prematurely. Shows like 'Waitress: The Musical' and 'Beetlejuice' (c...

item.title: Volkswagen Introduces New Electric SUV: ID. Cross
item.description: Volkswagen has launched the all-new ID. Cross, an electric compact SUV designed as an alternative to the T-Cross. Built on the MEB+ platform, the vehicle measures 4,153mm in length with a 2,601mm whee...

item.title: Shingles Vaccine Linked to Reduced Dementia Risk and Health Benefits
item.description: A recent study suggests that the shingles vaccine may offer additional health benefits beyond its primary purpose of preventing herpes zoster. Researchers found potential links between vaccination and...

item.title: DepEd Conducts First Active Shooter Drill to Enhance School Safety After Recent Shooting
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) conducted its first active shooter drill at Manila Science High School on July 15, 2026, as part of efforts to improve school safety and prevent campus violence. Ov...

item.title: Nicki Minaj Faces Legal Action Over Unpaid Legal Fees
item.description: A law firm that represented Nicki Minaj in a 2023 copyright case has filed a lawsuit against her in Los Angeles County Superior Court, claiming she owes KSh 29 million in unpaid legal fees. The firm, ...

item.title: Pakistan Enhances Security Measures Around Saindak Mine Amid Terror Threats
item.description: State Minister for Interior Tallal Chaudhry announced heightened security measures around the Saindak copper and gold mine in Balochistan following terrorist disruptions to supply routes. The mine's o...

item.title: Spain's Tactical Mastery Defeats France in World Cup Semi-Final – Stephanie Shaakaa
item.description: Spain’s 2-0 victory over France in the 2026 World Cup semi-final at Dallas Stadium was a masterclass in tactical dominance. The Spanish midfield trio of Rodri, Fabián Ruiz, and Dani Olmo controlled th...

item.title: Universities Struggle with Defining Antisemitism Amid Complex Debates
item.description: Australian universities face challenges in agreeing on a single definition of antisemitism, as mandated by new federal guidelines. The government requires institutions to adopt their own definitions t...

item.title: Bank of Canada maintains interest rates at 2.25% amid inflationary pressures
item.description: The Bank of Canada is set to deliver its fifth interest rate decision of the year, with economists widely expecting the central bank to keep its policy rate at 2.25%. Inflation has risen above three p...

item.title: ICTSI Achieves P2-Trillion Market Capitalization
item.description: The market capitalization of International Container Terminal Services, Inc. (ICTSI) has surpassed the P2-trillion mark, marking a significant milestone in the company's history. This achievement was ...

item.title: The Science Behind Winter Fatigue and Daylight Saving Time
item.description: The article explores the phenomenon of increased fatigue during winter months, linking it to the reduction in daylight hours and the impact of daylight saving time changes. It discusses how shorter da...

item.title: Impeachment court plans to investigate OVP and DepEd fund allegations by August 2049
item.description: The impeachment court is set to address allegations of misusing confidential funds from the Office of the Vice President (OVP) and Department of Education (DepEd) before August 2049. The case, which h...
